Inspect or modify content in the project (posts, pages)

Usage:
  nectar content [--config <path>] [--kind <posts|pages>] [--draft] [--tag <slug>] [--author <slug>] [--json] [--redirect] <subcommand...>

Arguments:
  subcommand          `list` (show posts/pages) or `rename <old-slug> <new-slug>` (move a post/page file + rewrite its `slug` frontmatter)

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  --kind <posts|pages>  For `list`: filter by content kind (posts or pages). For `rename`: which kind to look up the slug under (defaults to posts; pass `pages` to rename a page slug instead)
  --draft             Include draft posts/pages in the listing (default: only published; `list` only)
  --tag <slug>        Show only entries that have any given tag slug (`list` only); repeat or comma-separate
  --author <slug>     Show only entries that have any given author slug (`list` only); repeat or comma-separate
  -j, --json          Emit results as JSON for CI consumption (both `list` and `rename`)
  --redirect          On `rename`: append a `<old-url>  <new-url>  301` entry to `redirects.yaml` at the project root so the old URL keeps working when emitted through the redirects component
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → NECTAR_CONTENT_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  nectar content list                          # posts + pages with status/date
  nectar content list --kind pages
  nectar content list --tag changelog --json
  nectar content rename old-slug new-slug --redirect
